title: pelargonium citronellum
intro: Pelargonium citronellum, also known as the Rosalina or Cape Lavender, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the Western Cape region of South Africa. This plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects, making it useful in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of Pelargonium citronellum include its ability to soothe respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce stress and promote a good night's sleep. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, essential oils, and capsules to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
